title.es: Hacer visible el trabajo de alguien

question.en: Tell me about someone whose work you made sure got seen by the right people. What did you do, and what did you NOT claim for yourself?
question.es: Cuéntame sobre alguien cuyo trabajo te aseguraste de que fuera visto por las personas correctas. ¿Qué hiciste, y qué fue lo que NO reclamaste para ti mismo?

must_include: who the person was and why their work was at risk of going unseen — the visibility gap the candidate identified

the specific action the candidate took to direct credit toward that person — a concrete action (sent an email naming them, mentioned them in a review, corrected attribution in a presentation) not a vague gesture
who witnessed or received the credit-giving — credit distribution only counts if it reaches someone with influence or visibility over the contributor's career or reputation
at least one thing the candidate explicitly did NOT claim for themselves — the renunciation of credit is the RFI signal, not just the giving of credit
whether the person being credited knew the candidate had done this — and the candidate's reasoning about whether to tell them or not
must_avoid: credit-hoarding with a modesty veneer: 'we did it as a team, but really I was the one driving most of it' — this is not credit-giving, it is credit-sharing after retaining the majority
framing the credit-giving as a strategic move ('I knew it would make me look like a good leader') rather than as something owed to the contributor
vague language about 'making sure people got recognition' without naming the person, the contribution, or the specific act of attribution
omitting what the candidate gave up — if the candidate lost nothing by giving credit, the story is probably not an RFI story
